decks.notes: The deck focuses on disrupting opponent's resources while quickly deploying aggressive threats to gain advantage. By using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board, along with creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to pressure the opponent, the deck aims to outpace and outvalue the opponent. Cards like Solitude and Flickerwisp offer additional disruptive elements, while Teferi, Time Raveler provides flexibility and protection. The mana base is designed to support the deck's strategy and enable consistent access to the necessary colors of mana.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Atraxa and Griselbrand, using cards like Faithful Mending and Goryo's Vengeance to bring them back into play. Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog provide card selection and disruption, while Solitude and Force of Negation help protect our combo pieces. Teferi, Time Raveler can disrupt opponents and allow for combo turns, while Ephemerate provides additional value. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and disrupt opponents' game plans to achieve a swift victory. The land base is designed to support the deck's multicolor requirements.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creatures and burn spells to deal damage quickly and efficiently. With a mix of powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, coupled with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early on and finish them off before they can stabilize. Supporting cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Voice of Victory provide additional value and options for the deck. The inclusion of cards like Mana Tithe and Thraben Charm help disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining pressure.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By playing powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer, along with efficient removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. With cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ocelot Pride providing additional support and buffs, the deck aims to close out the game swiftly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. Additionally, cards like Arena of Glory and Voice of Victory provide ways to further enhance your creatures and potentially give you an edge in combat.

This deck focuses on assembling the Allosaurus Rider combo using Eldritch Evolution and Neoform to tutor for key pieces like Generous Ent, Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. The goal is to generate a large amount of mana through cards like Disciple of Freyalise and Wistfulness to cast spells like Griselbrand and Xenagos, God of Revels to finish the game. The deck also includes utility cards like Consign to Memory and Pact of Negation for protection, as well as Planar Genesis for board presence. The mana base supports the combo with fetch lands and utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by countering their spells, removing their creatures, and controlling the board while generating card advantage through card draw and library manipulation. The deck focuses on maintaining control of the game through counterspells, removal spells like Fatal Push and Sheoldred's Edict, and utilizing Wan Shi Tong, Librarian for card advantage. The mana base is designed to support both blue and black spells, while also providing access to Field of Ruin for additional disruption.

The deck focuses on generating as much mana as possible to cast spells that will create a storm count and deal damage to the opponent. By using cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage, Ruby Medallion, and rituals like Desperate Ritual and Pyretic Ritual, the deck aims to chain together spells to generate a high storm count. Cards like Highway Robbery and Grapeshot can then be used to deal significant damage to the opponent in a single turn. The deck includes card draw and spell recursion to help maintain momentum and close out the game quickly.

This deck focuses on casting multiple spells in one turn to generate a large amount of mana and cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot. The key strategy is to create a storm of spells through cards like Desperate Ritual and Pyretic Ritual while utilizing card draw and selection with Wrenn's Resolve and Reckless Impulse. The deck also includes Past in Flames to recast spells from the graveyard and finish off opponents with a burst of damage. The land base is optimized for red mana generation to support the combo-heavy playstyle.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts and generating value from them while pressuring the opponent with aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards like Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble to generate card advantage and mana acceleration, along with efficient removal options like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and powerful board presence. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Salvage Titan provide additional value by recurring artifacts from the graveyard. The deck's main strategy is to build a strong artifact-based board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize the Amulet of Vigor to ramp up mana quickly and efficiently in order to play big threats like Primeval Titan. The deck focuses on setting up powerful combos that allow for explosive plays, such as using Scapeshift to search for multiple lands and win the game in one turn. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Urza's Saga and Boseiju, Who Endures help to support the deck's overall strategy and provide additional utility. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its quick and explosive plays.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing a combination of powerful creatures and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. With cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls leading the charge, supported by aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, this deck aims to put pressure on opponents early and often. Combining burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment with powerful creatures, this deck looks to finish the game quickly while controlling the board with cards like Thraben Charm and Ranger-Captain of Eos.

This deck focuses on utilizing powerful combo pieces and control elements to win the game without relying on traditional lands. By utilizing cards like Goblin Charbelcher and Lotus Bloom, the deck aims to quickly ramp up mana and combo off for a win. Disrupting the opponent's plans with cards like Disrupting Shoal and Sink into Stupor while also having access to counterspells like Flare of Denial and Force of Negation provides the necessary protection to execute the combo. The deck also contains a mix of versatile spells and utility lands to adapt to various situations.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts, particularly creatures with the artifact subtype, to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By using cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Arcbound Ravager, along with support cards like Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal, the deck aims to quickly build a board presence and apply pressure. The inclusion of cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke provide additional control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Urza's Saga and Weapons Manufacturing offer additional resources and threats. Ultimately, the deck aims to win through a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells.

The deck focuses on using efficient creatures with flash and enters-the-battlefield abilities to control the board while constantly drawing cards and generating mana through Birthing Ritual. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan through counterspells and bounce effects while deploying threats that can quickly close out the game. The combination of tempo plays and card advantage allows the deck to constantly apply pressure and keep the opponent off balance, making it difficult for them to establish a stable board presence.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cheap and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, along with a variety of burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Mishra's Bauble and Preordain helps to keep the deck's momentum going by providing card selection and card advantage. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure smooth mana development and consistent performance.

The focus of this deck is to utilize the UrzaTron lands to quickly ramp into powerful Eldrazi creatures and control spells. With a mix of Eldrazi creatures, control spells, and mana acceleration, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while deploying big threats like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger. By using Karn, the Great Creator to tutor for specific artifacts and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to wipe the board, this deck looks to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a prolonged game.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and burn spells to quickly overwhelm opponents. Utilizing powerful creatures like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, along with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Additionally, the inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional utility and resources to maintain pressure on opponents. The deck also includes a small disruptive element with Goblin Bombardment and Blood Moon to disrupt opponents' strategies.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, aiming to deal damage quickly and efficiently. It utilizes powerful creatures like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Phlage, along with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board and push for lethal damage. The inclusion of cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ranger-Captain of Eos provides additional value and utility. With a mix of creature aggression and burn spells, this Boros Aggro deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with low-cost aggressive creatures like Ragavan and Guide of Souls while utilizing powerful spells like Galvanic Discharge to control the board. The deck also includes versatile cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer for extra value and card advantage. With support from cards like Ocelot Pride and Voice of Victory, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature pressure and burn spells. Additionally, cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison disrupt the opponent's game plan, giving this deck a competitive edge in the Modern format.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively attack opponents with a combination of powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, supported by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck aims to control the board with cards like Ocelot Pride and Seasoned Pyromancer while using cards like Voice of Victory to boost its creatures. Its mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, allowing for quick access to the necessary colors for spells. The inclusion of cards like Static Prison and Thraben Charm provides additional utility and disruption against opponents.

The deck focuses on creating a powerful board presence through the use of creatures like Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker while utilizing spells like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble to control the opponent's board. The deck then aims to generate mana quickly to cast Emrakul, the Promised End or Walking Ballista to finish off the opponent. The inclusion of Urza's Saga and Eldrazi Temple helps accelerate the deck's strategy, while cards like Blade of the Bloodchief and Agatha's Soul Cauldron provide additional support for the combo win condition.

This Eldrazi Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures. By utilizing efficient creatures like Devourer of Destiny and Thought-Knot Seer, along with disruptive spells like Kozilek's Command, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain control throughout the game. The deck also includes cards like Chalice of the Void and Wastescape+Battlemage','','');">Wastescape Battlemage to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while Cavern of Souls and Eldrazi Temple provide mana acceleration to help cast the big Eldrazi threats. Overall, the deck aims to win quickly and decisively before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly using cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple to accelerate into powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. Control elements are included with cards like Kozilek's Command and Unholy Heat to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also utilizes a variety of lands to ensure consistent mana production and to support the Eldrazi theme. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast mana generation and powerful threats while maintaining control over the game.

The main idea of this deck is to ramp up mana quickly using cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple, allowing you to play powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker early in the game. In addition, the deck includes a variety of control elements such as Kozilek's Command and Sowing Mycospawn to disrupt your opponent's strategy. With a mix of ramp and control, the deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with large Eldrazi threats while keeping them off balance.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling a powerful combo using cards like Goblin Charbelcher and Lotus Bloom to deal massive amounts of damage to the opponent. The key strategy is to control the game with counterspells like Disrupting Shoal and Flare of Denial while ramping up mana with cards like Hydroelectric Specimen and Tameshi, Reality Architect. The deck doesn't rely on traditional lands, instead opting for alternative sources of mana like Sea Gate Restoration and Whir of Invention. Overall, the deck aims to win the game through a quick and explosive combo before the opponent can react.

This deck focuses on quickly playing artifacts and using them to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats. By combining cheap artifacts with cards like Arcbound Ravager and Engineered Explosives, the deck can control the board while still dealing damage. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recursion of key artifacts, while Metallic Rebuke provides counterplay options. Urza's Saga helps to tutor for important artifact pieces, making the deck consistent in its strategy. Overall, this deck is fast-paced and aims to close out the game quickly through its affinity for artifacts.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly swarm the board and control the opponent's resources. By combining aggressive creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary with powerful artifacts like Engineered Explosives and Mox Opal, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of early aggression and disruption. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps to recur artifacts from the graveyard, while cards like Tormod's Crypt and Metallic Rebuke provide additional disruption and protection. Urza's Saga serves as a powerful utility land that can search for key artifacts to further advance the deck's strategy.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggression. By combining powerful artifact creatures like Arcbound Ravager and Emry, Lurker of the Loch with cards like Engineered Explosives and Mishra's Bauble, the deck can quickly build a threatening board presence. With the help of cards like Urza's Saga and Metallica Rebuke, the deck can efficiently control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The synergy between artifacts and various support cards allows for a fast-paced and explosive gameplay experience.

Utilizing artifacts to quickly flood the board with creatures and quickly defeat opponents by overwhelming them with aggressive plays. The deck includes efficient affinity cards, artifact accelerants, disruption tools, and Urza's Saga as a powerful enabler. The main strategy is to constantly generate value through card draw and token generation, while also disrupting opponents with removal and counter spells. The goal is to quickly build a threatening board presence and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to generate a lot of mana efficiently using cards like Ruby Medallion and rituals, then cast a large number of spells in one turn to either deal direct damage with cards like Grapeshot or create a board state that overwhelms the opponent. The deck also aims to utilize Ral, Monsoon Mage's abilities to control the opponent's board while building up its own resources. The inclusion of Wrenn's Resolve and Past in Flames allows the player to recycle spells and maintain card advantage throughout the game.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combo potential of Splinter Twin and Deceiver Exarch to create an infinite loop of token creatures. By protecting the combo pieces with counterspells and removal spells, the deck aims to control the game until it can assemble the combo and win the game in a single explosive turn. The inclusion of Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian provides card advantage and selection to help find the combo pieces more quickly. It is a high-risk, high-reward strategy that can lead to quick wins if executed correctly.

The key concept of this deck is to use the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to ramp up mana quickly and play powerful Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers like Karn and Ugin. By controlling the board with removal spells and disruptive creatures like Thought-Knot Seer, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats and haymakers. The deck's goal is to establish early control of the game, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and ultimately close out the game with its powerful late-game threats.
